producer.name: Marchesi Antinori
producer.notes: One of Italy's oldest and most important wine producers. Pioneers of the Super Tuscan movement.

estates.name: Antinori nel Chianti Classico
estates.region: San Casciano Val di Pesa, Tuscany

estates.name: Tenuta Bocca di Lupo
estates.region: Castel del Monte, Minervino Murge, Puglia

estates.name: Stag's Leap Wine Cellars
estates.notes: Already in Loam DB as separate producer

wines.name: Tignanello

oak.type: French oak barrels, small percentage Hungarian oak

wines.notes: The original Super Tuscan. First Sangiovese aged in barriques, first red wine from Chianti Classico blended with non-traditional varieties (Cabernet), first to eliminate white grapes from Chianti blend.

wines.name: Solaia
wines.notes: Top-tier Super Tuscan, Cabernet-dominant. 1978-1979 was 80% Cab Sauv, 20% Cab Franc (no Sangiovese). Produced only in exceptional vintages.

wines.name: Marchese Antinori

grapes.note: dominant, with small percentage of complementary varieties

wines.notes: Estate's flagship Chianti Classico Riserva from Tenuta Tignanello vineyards.

wines.name: Badia a Passignano

oak.type: French oak, small portion Hungarian oak

wines.soil: Calcareous, rich in limestone with clay
wines.notes: Gran Selezione — the top tier of Chianti Classico hierarchy (above Riserva). 100% Sangiovese from the historic Badia a Passignano monastery estate.

wines.name: Pèppoli

oak.type: Slavonian oak barrels (large), small percentage in barriques

wines.notes: Entry-level Chianti Classico. Approachable, earlier-drinking style.

wines.name: Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva
wines.notes: Villa Antinori brand Chianti Classico Riserva.

wines.name: Villa Antinori Rosso
wines.notes: Flagship Villa Antinori red blend. IGT allows international grape variety inclusion.

wines.name: Villa Antinori Bianco
wines.notes: First produced 1931 by Marchese Niccolò Antinori. Originally Trebbiano-based, Chardonnay added in 1980s, now Pinot Grigio/Pinot Bianco/Vermentino.

wines.name: Villa Antinori Pinot Bianco
wines.notes: Grapes from Tenuta Monteloro in Fiesole.

wines.name: Guado al Tasso
wines.notes: Flagship Bordeaux blend from Bolgheri. DOC Superiore is the top tier of the Bolgheri appellation. Estate also grows Syrah, Grenache, Viognier, Sémillon.

wines.name: Matarocchio
wines.notes: Ultra-premium single-vineyard 100% Cabernet Franc. Produced only in exceptional vintages. One of Italy's finest varietal Cab Francs.

wines.name: Cont'Ugo
wines.notes: Single-variety Merlot from Guado al Tasso.

wines.name: Il Bruciato
wines.notes: Entry-level Bolgheri red. Also includes Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot in small quantities. 2003 not produced.

wines.name: Scalabrone
wines.notes: Rosato from Bolgheri. Named after a local bandit from the 1800s. Short maceration (2-4 hours).

wines.name: Vermentino
wines.notes: 100% Vermentino, indigenous Tyrrhenian coast variety.

wines.name: Cervaro della Sala
wines.notes: Italy's most celebrated white wine. One of the first Italian whites to undergo malolactic fermentation and barrique aging. Chardonnay fermented in barriques, Grechetto fermented separately without oak.

wines.name: Pinot Nero della Sala
wines.soil: Calcareous with Pliocene-epoch sedimentary sand fossils
wines.notes: Single-variety Pinot Noir from high-altitude Umbrian vineyards.

wines.name: Bramìto della Sala
wines.notes: Entry-level Chardonnay from Castello della Sala. Partial oak fermentation, only oak-fermented lots undergo MLF.

wines.name: Conte della Vipera
wines.soil: Sedimentary marine fossils with clay veins
wines.notes: Sauvignon Blanc/Sémillon blend. No MLF (prevented by cold temperature control at 10°C).

wines.name: San Giovanni della Sala
wines.notes: Traditional Orvieto blend. Procanico is the local name for Trebbiano Toscano.

wines.name: Muffato della Sala
wines.notes: Botrytized sweet wine. All grapes affected by Botrytis cinerea (noble rot). Original blend: 50% Sauvignon Blanc, 30% Grechetto, 20% Drupeggio. Composition has varied over years. Named after 'muffa nobile' (noble rot).

wines.name: Pian delle Vigne
wines.notes: 100% Sangiovese (Brunello clone). Large-format oak barrels, 2 years minimum as per DOCG regulations.

wines.name: Pian delle Vigne Vignaferrovia
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Brunello Riserva. 4-hectare plot adjacent to 19th-century railway station. Produced only in top vintages.

wines.name: Pian delle Vigne Rosso di Montalcino
wines.notes: Junior wine of Brunello di Montalcino. Shorter aging requirement.

wines.name: La Braccesca
wines.notes: Estate Vino Nobile. Traditional Sangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile clone) with Merlot.

wines.name: Vigneto Santa Pia
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Vino Nobile from the Santa Pia sub-zone.

wines.name: Maggiarino
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Vino Nobile from the Maggiarino sub-zone.

wines.name: Sabazio
wines.notes: Junior wine of Vino Nobile. Shorter aging, stainless steel only.

wines.name: Bramasole
wines.notes: 100% Syrah from Cortona. One of Italy's benchmark Syrahs.

wines.name: Achelo
wines.notes: Entry-level Syrah from Cortona. More approachable than Bramasole.

wines.name: Poggio alle Nane
wines.notes: Top wine from Le Mortelle estate. Cab Franc/Cab Sauv/Carménère blend — unusual inclusion of Carménère.

wines.name: Botrosecco
wines.notes: Mid-range red from Le Mortelle. Cabernet blend.

wines.name: Ampio delle Mortelle
wines.notes: Top wine from Le Mortelle. Features Carménère — rare in Italian wines.

wines.name: Vivia
wines.notes: White blend from Le Mortelle in Maremma. Vermentino/Viognier/Ansonica.

wines.name: Vie Cave
wines.notes: 100% Malbec grown on volcanic/tufaceous soils in Maremma. Named after Etruscan roads carved into tufa rock. Unusual variety for Italy.

wines.name: Aleatico
wines.notes: Sweet red wine. Part of harvest air-dried for ~1 month. Fermentation halted at optimal sugar levels. Rare variety.

wines.name: A
wines.notes: Rosé from 100% Aleatico. Short cold maceration (4-6 hours). Aromatic variety.

wines.name: Mezzo Braccio
wines.notes: 100% Riesling from Fiesole hills above Florence. Unusual variety for Tuscany. Extended bottle aging (24 months).

wines.name: Scabrezza
wines.notes: 100% Pinot Grigio from Fiesole hills.

wines.name: Cuvée Royale Brut
wines.notes: Metodo classico sparkling. 36 months on lees before disgorgement. Flagship non-vintage Franciacorta.

wines.name: Rosé
wines.notes: Rosé Franciacorta. 24 months on lees.

wines.name: Blanc de Blancs
wines.notes: Blanc de Blancs Franciacorta. 24 months on lees.

wines.name: Conte Aymo
wines.notes: Vintage-dated Franciacorta. Produced only in exceptional years.

wines.name: Contessa Maggi
wines.notes: Top-tier Franciacorta Riserva. 60 months on lees. Produced only in exceptional vintages.

wines.name: Donna Cora Satèn
wines.notes: Satèn style — lower pressure (max 5 atm vs 6 for standard), creamier texture. Vintage-dated.

wines.name: Bocca di Lupo
wines.notes: Flagship Aglianico from Puglia. Top wine of the Bocca di Lupo estate.

wines.name: Pietrabianca
wines.notes: Premium white from Puglia. 'In a land of red wines, an outstanding white.'

wines.name: Fiano di Bocca di Lupo
wines.notes: Apulian interpretation of Fiano.

wines.name: Ettore
wines.notes: 100% Nero di Troia — one of southern Italy's noblest indigenous varieties.

wines.name: Trentangeli
wines.notes: More approachable expression of Aglianico from Bocca di Lupo.

wines.name: Kaloro
wines.notes: Sweet Moscato from Trani. Bright acidity prevents excessive sweetness.

wines.name: Locone
wines.notes: Cabernet from Bocca di Lupo. Water-focused viticulture approach.

wines.name: Masseria Maìme
wines.notes: Flagship Negroamaro from Upper Salento.

wines.name: Torcicoda
wines.notes: First Apulian wine in Wine Spectator's Top 100.

wines.name: Carrubo
wines.notes: Primitivo di Manduria DOC — from over-ripe grapes.

wines.name: Calafuria
wines.notes: Rosato from Salento.

wines.name: Roycello
wines.notes: Fiano from Salento.

wines.name: Chardonnay
wines.notes: Puglia Chardonnay.

wines.name: Fichimori
wines.notes: Light red, meant to be served chilled. Cold pre-fermentation maceration.

wines.name: Furia
wines.notes: Rosato from Salento.

wines.name: Neprica Negroamaro
wines.notes: Entry-level Negroamaro.

wines.name: Neprica Primitivo
wines.notes: Entry-level Primitivo.

wines.name: Neprica Rosé
wines.notes: Entry-level Puglia rosato.

wines.name: Vigna Colonnello
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Barolo Riserva from Bussia MGA. Top wine of Prunotto.

wines.name: Bussia
wines.notes: Barolo from Bussia MGA in Monforte d'Alba.

wines.name: Cerretta
wines.notes: Barolo from Cerretta MGA in Serralunga d'Alba.

wines.name: Mosconi
wines.notes: Barolo from Mosconi MGA in Monforte d'Alba.

wines.name: Serra
wines.notes: Barolo from Serra MGA.

wines.name: Barolo
wines.notes: Entry-level/village Barolo blend.

wines.name: Bric Turot
wines.notes: Barbaresco from Bric Turot MGA in Treiso.

wines.name: Secondine
wines.notes: Barbaresco from Secondine MGA.

wines.name: Barbaresco
wines.notes: Entry-level/village Barbaresco blend.

wines.name: Occhetti
wines.notes: Langhe Nebbiolo — more approachable entry point to Nebbiolo.

wines.name: Costamiòle
wines.notes: Nizza DOCG Riserva — top-tier Barbera d'Asti from the Nizza subzone.

wines.name: Pian Romualdo
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Barbera d'Alba.

wines.name: Barbera d'Alba
wines.notes: Village-level Barbera d'Alba.

wines.name: Mosesco
wines.notes: Single-vineyard Dolcetto d'Alba.

wines.name: Dolcetto d'Alba
wines.notes: Village-level Dolcetto d'Alba.

wines.name: Bansella
wines.notes: Nizza DOCG Barbera.

wines.name: Bricco Colma
wines.notes: 100% Albarossa — a crossing of Nebbiolo and Barbera created by Prof. Dalmasso. Rare variety.

wines.name: Fiulot
wines.notes: Barbera d'Asti DOCG.

wines.name: Mompertone
wines.notes: Monferrato DOC red blend, typically Barbera/Syrah.

wines.name: Arveja Langhe Arneis
wines.notes: 100% Arneis from Roero area.

wines.name: Moscato d'Asti
wines.notes: Sweet frizzante Moscato d'Asti.

wines.name: Granadié
wines.notes: Langhe Rosato.

wines.name: Arveja
wines.notes: Langhe DOC white. Separate from the Arneis version.

schema_test_observations.classification_complexity: Tests DOCG vs DOC vs IGT at same producer. Tignanello/Solaia are IGT but premium-priced (Super Tuscan paradox). Badia a Passignano is DOCG Gran Selezione (highest tier).
schema_test_observations.designation_variety: Riserva (Marchese Antinori, Villa Antinori CC, Vignaferrovia, Vigna Colonnello, Costamiòle, Contessa Maggi), Gran Selezione (Badia a Passignano), Superiore (Guado al Tasso, Matarocchio, Aleatico), Satèn (Donna Cora), Millesimato/vintage (Conte Aymo), Classico Superiore (San Giovanni della Sala).
schema_test_observations.multi_estate: Single producer with 20+ estates across Tuscany, Umbria, Lombardy, Piedmont, Puglia — plus USA and Chile. Each estate has its own wine lineup.
schema_test_observations.grape_diversity: 33+ grape varieties across the portfolio. From Nebbiolo in Piedmont to Aglianico in Puglia to Carménère in Maremma.
schema_test_observations.super_tuscan_concept: Tignanello and Solaia are classified as IGT (lowest tier) but are among Italy's most expensive wines. Schema must handle IGT wines that are premium, not entry-level.
schema_test_observations.sweet_wines: Muffato della Sala (botrytized), Aleatico (passito-style), Moscato d'Asti (frizzante), Kaloro (Moscato di Trani) — different styles of sweet wine.
schema_test_observations.sparkling: 6 Franciacorta DOCG wines test traditional method sparkling with lees aging data.
